name: All-Purpose Chocolate Syrup (No Fat Added)
intro: Real chocolate syrup, made without a drop of added fat, belongs in the refrigerators of chocolate lovers at all times—standing at the ready for all the puddings, ice creams, and pieces of cake waiting for a healthy drizzle of chocolate.

ingredients.item: plus 6 tablespoons / 270 ml boiling water, divided
plus 1 tablespoon / 124 grams organic granulated sugar
pure maple syrup, Grade B or dark amber

method: Sift the cocoa powder into a medium saucepan. Pour ¾ cup / 180 ml of the boiling water over the cocoa. Stir with a silicone spatula to moisten the cocoa. (The paste need not be smooth.) Remove from the heat and set aside for 5 minutes while the cocoa blooms.

Whisk the remaining 6 tablespoons / 90 ml of very hot water into the cocoa paste and add a pinch of salt. Cook over low heat, stirring with the silicone spatula for 2 minutes, making sure to reach the bottom and sides of the saucepan.

Add the sugar and maple syrup, and continue to cook over lowest heat for another minute. Taste the syrup carefully: it will be hot. If you want sweeter syrup, add another tablespoon each of sugar and water. When small bubbles start to form around the sides, it is time to turn off the heat. Stir the vanilla into the syrup.
Cool the syrup to room temperature in the saucepan, stirring once or twice while the syrup cools.
